name: Guadeloupe (France)

colors.symbolism: the blue chief of the arms, evoking the Caribbean sky and sea
the field of the arms; the volcanic soil of Guadeloupe
the three gold fleurs-de-lis of French royal heraldry
the sun and sugar cane symbolising the islands' agricultural wealth

description: A horizontally divided field — narrow blue stripe at the top above a wider black field — charged with three gold fleurs-de-lis on the blue chief and a green sun rising over a green sugar cane on the black field.
history: Guadeloupe's official flag is the French Tricolore; this local flag, derived from the historic coat of arms of Basse-Terre (the capital), is in widespread unofficial use as a Guadeloupean territorial emblem. Guadeloupe became a French overseas department in 1946 and an overseas region in 1982; it remains an integral part of the French Republic and the European Union. A separate unofficial flag based on a sun-and-cyclone design is also used.

features.symbolism: the design derives from the historic arms of Basse-Terre, capital of Guadeloupe
the three fleurs-de-lis recall Guadeloupe's status as a former French royal colony
the rising sun and a stalk of sugar cane representing the islands' tropical climate and the historic importance of sugar cultivation
